element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fighter (strong melee), Magician (strong magic), Saint (hazard navigation) - each class defines early playstyle constraints and weapon availability
Free navigation between screens across forests, deserts, towns; establishes spatial literacy for entire game
Sword for close-range, rod for projectiles; foundational combat during exploration
Quest initiation, party recruitment, shop purchases, HP/MP recovery at hotels, saves at mosques
Fight/Escape/Bribe options; turn trading with single or up to 2 allies; distinct from real-time combat
Slaying enemies yields XP; leveling increases HP/MP maxima and can unlock new spells/weapon strength
Learning formations at Magic Universities for party defense/group spells; spell casting in turn-based battles
Transport to past/present/future versions of same location; environmental variation across time
Forests, deserts, underwater areas, dimly-lit dungeons, palace labyrinths introduce spatial/visual variety
Hired units aid party attacks in turn-based battles; adds tactical layer to party composition
Find palace, defeat resident demon as chapter objective; escalating difficulty per chapter
Culmination of all learned mechanics; endgame confrontation

typical_relief_triggers: Arriving in town (safe space for shopping, healing, information)
typical_tension_triggers: Palace labyrinth navigation toward chapter bosses
Demon boss fights at chapter conclusions
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Chapter-based structure creates macro rhythm: exploration and grinding (moderate tension) → town resource management (relief) → dungeon delving (escalating tension) → boss confrontation (peak tension) → story advancement (relief). Time gate mechanic provides optional pacing variation.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Time Gates allow optional exploration of past/present/future versions of same location; mercenary hiring and formation learning in towns are optional optimizations

difficulty_curve.early_game: Chapter 1 introduces combat mechanics gradually; class selection determines early weapon/ability balance; real-time and turn-based combat introduced in low-threat context
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Chapters 2-3 expand biome variety and environmental complexity; time travel mechanics expand spatial puzzle layer; demon boss difficulty escalates
difficulty_curve.late_game: Chapter 4 culminates pre-final exploration with enhanced demon threats; Chapter 5 focuses entirely on Sabaron final boss
notable_spikes: First demon boss (Chapter 1) - establishes turn-based battle difficulty baseline
Underwater/dungeon environments - introduce environmental hazard types and reduced visibility
Successive chapter bosses - incremental stat/skill requirements
Sabaron final battle - aggregate mechanical mastery required

notable_level_patterns.description: Five-chapter progression where each chapter (1-4) requires: overworld exploration → locate palace → defeat chapter demon; Chapter 5 is pure boss focus. Enables clear progression milestones and narrative punctuation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Chapter 1: Explore biome → find demon palace → defeat Goragora-aligned demon → story progression

notable_level_patterns.description: Towns serve as safe hubs (NPCs, shops, healing, class changes, saves) interspersed with dangerous exploration areas (overworld, dungeons, labyrinths). Creates tension/relief pacing rhythm.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Desert town (relief) → adjacent dungeon exploration (tension) → return to town for recovery

notable_level_patterns.description: Same overworld location accessible in past/present/future versions via Time Gates. Enables revisiting areas with new mechanics/threats without traditional backtracking penalty.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Chapter 2-3: Revisit early location in future version; environment and enemy composition differ; new secrets/upgrades available

notable_level_patterns.description: Each chapter concludes with navigating a palace labyrinth to confront chapter demon. Increases spatial complexity and danger density toward chapter conclusion.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Chapter 4: Extended palace maze with high enemy frequency → Demon boss chamber
